title: P0 expert-overlap U: 35B-A3B speculative decode IS bandwidth-viable
method: Captured the TARGET verifier's per-layer routed-expert selections for the B+1 rows of REAL verify cycles (root + MTP-head drafts) via FP16/BF16 router monkey-patch (diagnostic only; HIPENGINE_MOE_C1_C_DISPATCH=0 forces the Python router path -- selections are dispatch-independent). BW floor = (1-f)+f*U, U=unique_experts/top_k; byte-weighted floor uses actual per-layer non-expert + unique*expert bytes from the safetensors header.

skipped.reason: rows=8 not realizable by the native MTP proposer (allowed budgets {1,2,3,5})

verdict.summary: 35B-A3B speculative decode is structurally BANDWIDTH-VIABLE at every realizable budget: visible_tokens_per_cycle > BW floor for B in {1,2,3,5}. It is NOT a research-only / persistent-kernel project on bandwidth grounds.
verdict.b_sweet_spot: B=3 has the best headroom (+0.88). B=5 headroom shrinks (+0.38) because visible saturates/drops (2.30 < B=3's 2.556, acceptance saturated) while the floor keeps rising -- matches #100's B=3-optimal. Higher B costs more expert bytes without more accepted tokens.
verdict.U_scaling: U scales SUBLINEARLY with rows (dedup grows 14%->44% across B=1->5): experts partially overlap, more so at higher B. But root-vs-draft Jaccard is LOW (~0.18-0.21) -- root and drafts route to mostly DIFFERENT experts; the dedup is mostly among adjacent draft rows. f_mean approximation matches byte-weighted floor (1.677 vs 1.675).
verdict.binding_constraint: Bandwidth is NOT the binding constraint (floor 1.3-1.9 << visible 1.8-2.6). Per #101 the verify is DISPATCH-bound (~40us/launch host gap), so M14.dispatch.1 (C-side dispatcher) -- not expert bandwidth -- is the lever.

grouped_expert_gemm_decision.exists: run_moe_grouped_compact_fp16 already implemented; verify path FORCES c=1 (per-row) MoE because grouped's fixed setup cost exceeds c=1 at small B+1.
grouped_expert_gemm_decision.decision: DEFERRED, not built-now. Dedup reaches 44% / 1.83x duplicate-savings at B=5, but (a) the verify is dispatch-bound not BW-bound (#101), so saving expert BYTES does not help wall time now; (b) grouped's fixed cost > c=1 at the small B+1 verify shapes. Revisit ONLY after M14.dispatch.1 lowers the dispatch floor AND at higher B where 44% dedup justifies the grouped fixed cost.

forecast_seed_106.note: marginal-row BW term = f*dU; viable since floor << visible. Binding constraint is dispatch (#101), not bandwidth.
